title: salix × fragilis
intro: Salix × fragilis, also known as the crack willow, is a hybrid species of willow tree that is commonly found in wetlands and riparian areas. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating a variety of health issues. The bark of the Salix × fragilis contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which has been used to treat fever, pain, and inflammation, and has also been shown to have antioxidant and cardiovascular health benefits. The plant can be prepared as a salve, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in herbal remedies to treat conditions such as arthritis, gout, and digestive issues.
